title: Russian missile strike on Dnipro kills civilians
detail: A Russian missile attack on Dnipro hit civilian areas and infrastructure, part of the sustained nationwide pressure campaign on Ukrainian cities.
dl: MODERATE — Civilian districts damaged

title: Ukraine drone attack targets Tatarstan industrial facilities
detail: Long-range Ukrainian drones struck deep inside Russia, underscoring Kyiv's focus on oil, industrial, and military-linked targets beyond the front.
dl: LIGHT — Deep-strike disruption inside Russia

title: Russian glide bombs and missiles hit Kharkiv region
detail: Russian aviation and missiles intensified attacks on Kharkiv region, continuing the pattern of cross-border bombardment and pressure on northeastern Ukraine.
dl: MODERATE — Urban and energy damage mounting

title: Russia opens new push in Kharkiv border zone
detail: Russian forces pressed attacks in the Kharkiv border area, threatening settlements north of the city and forcing evacuations.
dl: HEAVY — Frontier settlements contested

title: Major Russian strike wave hits Kyiv and central Ukraine
detail: Russia launched one of its larger overnight drone and missile barrages against Kyiv and multiple regions, damaging homes and critical infrastructure.
dl: HEAVY — Capital struck in mass barrage

title: Ukraine hits Russian oil depot in border region
detail: Ukrainian drones targeted a Russian fuel and logistics site in support of Kyiv's campaign against oil-linked war infrastructure.
dl: MODERATE — Fuel storage disrupted

title: Russian strike damages Odesa port facilities
detail: Russian drones and missiles hit Odesa region, including port-linked infrastructure central to Ukraine's maritime exports.
dl: HEAVY — Export infrastructure damaged

title: Russia pounds Kyiv in large missile and drone attack
detail: A broad Russian aerial assault hit Kyiv and other cities, continuing Moscow's strategy of exhausting Ukrainian air defence and striking urban systems.
dl: HEAVY — Residential and utility damage

title: Ukraine drones hit Russian Black Sea and energy targets
detail: Kyiv expanded attacks on Russian military and fuel targets connected to the southern war effort and Black Sea operations.
dl: MODERATE — Naval and energy disruption

title: Ukraine launches cross-border incursion into Kursk region
detail: Ukrainian forces crossed into Russia's Kursk region in a surprise operation, seizing villages and forcing Moscow to divert troops.
dl: CRITICAL — Frontline penetrated inside Russia

title: Russia counterattacks against Ukrainian positions in Kursk
detail: Russian troops and aviation counterattacked in Kursk as Moscow sought to contain Ukraine's incursion and restore control over border settlements.
dl: HEAVY — Counteroffensive underway

title: Russia launches one of biggest attacks on Ukrainian energy grid
detail: A mass Russian missile and drone strike targeted power infrastructure across Ukraine, causing outages and renewed strain on the grid.
dl: CRITICAL — Nationwide power infrastructure hit

title: Ukraine strikes Moscow-area and refinery targets with drones
detail: Ukrainian drones targeted Moscow-region air defences and Russian oil infrastructure as Kyiv sustained pressure on rear-area logistics.
dl: MODERATE — Airspace and fuel systems pressured

title: Russia launches 595 drones and 48 missiles at Ukraine
detail: One of the largest reported Russian aerial attacks of the war targeted Kyiv and multiple regions with hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles.
dl: CRITICAL — Massive nationwide strike wave

title: Russian forces advance in Donetsk sector
detail: Russian troops continued grinding offensives in Donetsk, threatening key Ukrainian logistics towns and compounding pressure on the eastern front.
dl: HEAVY — Frontline pressure intensifies

title: Ukraine attacks Russian refinery and arms-linked sites
detail: Kyiv carried out another long-range drone wave against refinery and military-industrial targets inside Russia.
dl: MODERATE — Refining capacity disrupted

title: Russia renews sweeping attacks on Ukrainian power and cities
detail: A fresh nationwide strike package hit electricity and civilian systems as winter approached, reviving blackout concerns.
dl: CRITICAL — Winter energy campaign escalates

title: Ukraine drones hit Russian oil and military depots
detail: Ukrainian strikes again targeted Russia's oil-fed war economy and ammunition support network.
dl: MODERATE — Rear-area depots disrupted

title: Escalating Russian strikes target Odesa ports and bridge links
detail: Russia intensified attacks on Odesa-region maritime infrastructure in an effort to squeeze Ukraine's sea access and trade flows.
dl: HEAVY — Port throughput threatened

title: Russian air attack causes outages across Ukraine
detail: Russian forces hit energy and military facilities in another coordinated strike, sparking sweeping outages and continuing winter pressure on civilians.
dl: HEAVY — Blackout pressure renewed

title: Ukraine strikes Russian oil and gas facilities
detail: Ukraine hit Russian energy facilities with drones and Western-supplied weapons in a bid to reduce revenues funding the war.
dl: HEAVY — Energy revenue targets hit

title: Russian drones and missiles pound Ukraine before diplomacy push
detail: Russia launched another heavy aerial attack as diplomatic efforts stalled, illustrating Moscow's reliance on pressure through long-range strikes.
dl: HEAVY — Air defence saturation continues

title: Ukraine sustains pressure on Russian rear-area infrastructure
detail: Kyiv continued its deep-strike campaign against fuel, rail, and storage targets that support Russian operations at the front.
dl: MODERATE — Logistics friction increased

title: Russia batters Zaporizhzhia and frontline cities
detail: Russian missiles, drones and glide bombs hit southeastern Ukrainian cities and frontline communities under persistent pressure.
dl: MODERATE — Civilian and infrastructure damage

title: Russian forces keep grinding forward in Donbas
detail: Russia maintained incremental advances around eastern strongpoints while coupling ground assaults with air and artillery pressure.
dl: HEAVY — Attritional offensives continue

title: Ukraine hits Moscow region in large drone raid
detail: A large Ukrainian drone operation targeted the Moscow region and other sites, signaling Kyiv's ability to hold politically sensitive Russian areas at risk.
dl: MODERATE — Capital region disrupted

title: Russian mass drone attack hits central and western Ukraine
detail: Russia launched another broad drone wave against multiple Ukrainian regions, keeping steady pressure on infrastructure and civilians.
dl: MODERATE — Distributed nationwide damage

title: Russian attacks continue as front and air war grind on
detail: Russia maintained pressure with drone, missile and frontline attacks across Ukraine while Kyiv continued to absorb and answer with long-range strikes.
dl: MODERATE — War remains in sustained high-intensity phase

title: Easter ceasefire announced as Kyiv presses for longer pause
detail: Putin announced a 32-hour Orthodox Easter ceasefire and Zelenskiy said Ukraine would observe it, while Kyiv urged Moscow to extend the pause and restart talks rather than resume attacks after the holiday.
dl: LIGHT — Short humanitarian truce announced amid stalled peace efforts

title: Ukraine and Russia swap 175 POWs each before Easter truce
detail: Kyiv and Moscow exchanged 175 prisoners of war each, one of the few concrete outcomes linked to U.S.-brokered contacts, with Ukraine also receiving seven civilians and Russia taking back seven civilians from Kursk region.
dl: LIGHT — Major prisoner exchange completed

title: Easter truce quickly frays amid border drone strikes and front-line fire
detail: Hours after the ceasefire began, both sides accused each other of violations. Russian officials reported Ukrainian drone strikes in Kursk and Belgorod, while Ukraine's military said Russian forces committed hundreds of truce breaches including assaults, shelling and drone attacks.
dl: MODERATE — Ceasefire undermined almost immediately

title: Orthodox Easter ceasefire collapses under widespread shelling and drone attacks
detail: Russia and Ukraine accused each other of breaching the 32-hour Easter truce, but Ukrainian military reporting described thousands of Russian violations by the time the pause expired, including artillery fire, assault actions and attack-drone strikes across the front while civilians were reported hurt in Kharkiv region.
dl: MODERATE — Holiday truce broke down almost completely before expiry

title: Ukrainian drone strike sparks fires at Cherepovets Azot chemical plant
detail: Geolocated footage and regional reporting indicated a Ukrainian long-range drone strike set fires at the Cherepovets Azot plant in Vologda Oblast, part of Kyiv's continuing campaign against Russian industrial infrastructure supporting the war effort.
dl: MODERATE — Deep-rear industrial target hit in northwestern Russia

title: Ukrainian forces make a localized advance in the Slovyansk direction
detail: The Institute for the Study of War assessed that Ukrainian forces recently advanced on the Slovyansk axis, signaling continued counterpressure on a sector where Russia has been trying to build momentum toward the Slovyansk-Kramatorsk line.
dl: LIGHT — Local Ukrainian gain on the eastern front

title: Russian troops regain ground in the Oleksandrivka sector near the oblast tri-border
detail: ISW assessed that Russian forces recently advanced in the Oleksandrivka direction along the Zaporizhzhia-Dnipropetrovsk-Donetsk border area, showing Moscow can still recover some ground even as Ukrainian counterattacks have disrupted this sector in recent weeks.
dl: MODERATE — Russian localized advance on the southeastern front

title: Russian missile strike kills civilians in Dnipro
detail: A Russian missile strike on Dnipro killed at least five civilians, wounded more than two dozen people, and damaged transport-linked infrastructure and nearby civilian property in the southeastern city.
dl: MODERATE — Civilian traffic and infrastructure hit in a daytime strike

title: Ukraine and Germany sign major air-defence and drone production package
detail: Kyiv and Berlin signed a new defence package worth about €4 billion, including several hundred Patriot missiles, additional IRIS-T launchers, financing for deep-strike capabilities, and joint production of thousands of mid-strike drones.
dl: LIGHT — Major military aid and joint-production package agreed

title: Russia launches 324 drones and missiles in mass overnight barrage
detail: Russia launched 324 drones and three ballistic missiles against Ukraine overnight, with impacts reported in Odesa region port infrastructure, Dnipro, Cherkasy, Zaporizhzhia, and the Kyiv region despite extensive Ukrainian interceptions.
dl: HEAVY — Port facilities and multiple cities hit in a countrywide barrage

title: Ukraine unveils drone-assault combat model and says it retook territory in March
detail: Ukraine's Defence Ministry said it is introducing integrated drone-assault units that combine aerial and ground unmanned systems with infantry, and Commander-in-Chief Oleksandr Syrskyi said Ukrainian forces regained nearly 50 square kilometres of territory in March while disrupting Russia's spring offensive pressure.
dl: LIGHT — Ukraine reports localized territorial recovery and a new combat model

title: Ukrainian drone strike reaches Sterlitamak petrochemical plant in Bashkortostan
detail: ISW reported that Ukrainian forces struck the Sterlitamak Petrochemical Plant in Bashkortostan, roughly 1,500 kilometres from the border, as part of Kyiv's long-range campaign against Russian defence-industrial and fuel-related infrastructure.
dl: MODERATE — Deep strike on Russian petrochemical infrastructure

title: Britain announces its largest-ever annual drone package for Ukraine
detail: ISW reported that the United Kingdom will provide at least 120,000 drones to Ukraine in 2026, including long-range strike, reconnaissance, logistics, and maritime systems, with deliveries already beginning in April.
dl: LIGHT — Major allied drone-supply expansion announced

title: Ukrainian drones strike Tuapse port and oil infrastructure on Russia's Black Sea coast
detail: Ukraine struck infrastructure in and around Russia's Black Sea port of Tuapse, where officials reported fatalities, a fire, damage in the port area, and impacts involving oil-linked facilities. Reuters and ISW both identified Tuapse as part of Ukraine's continuing long-range campaign against Russian fuel and logistics infrastructure supporting the war effort.
dl: HEAVY — Major Black Sea port and oil hub struck in Russia

title: Russia's deadliest aerial attack of 2026 hits Kyiv, Odesa and Dnipro
detail: Russia launched more than 700 drones and missiles during a prolonged strike series, killing at least 17 people and wounding about 100 across Ukraine, with some of the heaviest damage in Kyiv, Odesa, and Dnipro. Ukrainian and ISW reporting highlighted the scale of the barrage and Russia's tactic of using layered waves to exhaust air defences before ballistic missile strikes.
dl: CRITICAL — Worst nationwide strike wave of the year hits major cities

title: Russian drones hit Izmail's Danube port and breach Romanian airspace
detail: A Russian overnight attack damaged administrative, production, and railway infrastructure at Izmail, Ukraine's largest Danube River port, while Romanian radar tracked one drone crossing briefly into Romanian airspace near the border. The strike extended several consecutive days of pressure on Ukraine's southwest logistics corridor.
dl: MODERATE — Danube logistics hub hit again and NATO-border airspace violated

title: Ukrainian forces make localized gains near Dobropillya and Slovyansk
detail: ISW assessed that Ukrainian forces recently advanced near Slovyansk and in the Dobropillya tactical area, indicating continued localized counterpressure on key eastern approaches as Russia sustains its broader Donetsk offensive effort.
dl: LIGHT — Local Ukrainian advance on the eastern front

title: Moscow escalates warnings over European drone production for Ukraine
detail: The Kremlin and former president Dmitry Medvedev publicly warned that expanding European drone manufacturing and supply chains for Ukraine could make related facilities potential Russian military targets, marking a sharper rhetorical escalation against Europe-based defence support for Kyiv.
dl: LIGHT — Russia sharpens threats against Europe-based support for Ukraine

title: Russian drone wave damages Odesa port infrastructure and knocks out power in Chernihiv region
detail: Russia launched 219 long-range drones overnight, damaging port infrastructure, warehouses, depots, and administrative buildings in Ukraine's Odesa region while also hitting an energy facility in Chernihiv region and causing blackouts for about 380,000 consumers. The strike extended Moscow's pressure campaign against Ukrainian logistics and power infrastructure after the larger nationwide barrages earlier in the week.
dl: MODERATE — Port and power infrastructure hit in renewed overnight drone attack

title: Ukrainian drones hit Samara refineries, Crimea fuel depot, and Baltic export port
detail: Ukrainian long-range drones struck multiple oil and fuel-linked targets across Russia and occupied Crimea, including refineries at Novokuibyshevsk and Syzran in Samara region, a fuel tank fire in Sevastopol, and the Lukoil terminal at Vysotsk port near St Petersburg. Reuters reported the attacks as part of Kyiv's intensified campaign against Russian oil logistics and export infrastructure that supports Moscow's war budget.
dl: HEAVY — Coordinated deep strike hits Russian oil export and refining network

title: Ukrainian drones hit Tuapse port again, reigniting fires at a key Black Sea oil hub
detail: A renewed Ukrainian drone attack struck Russia's Black Sea port of Tuapse only hours after an earlier blaze had been extinguished, killing at least one person, damaging transport infrastructure, and setting the port area on fire again. Reuters described Tuapse as a major oil product export hub and home to a Rosneft refinery, underscoring Kyiv's continuing campaign against Russian fuel and logistics infrastructure.
dl: MODERATE — Follow-on strike re-hits a major Russian Black Sea export and refining node

title: Russia says its forces are pressing into Kostiantynivka and closing on the Donbas fortress belt
detail: Russia's top general Valery Gerasimov said Russian forces had seized 1,700 square km of Ukrainian territory since the start of 2026, were already fighting in parts of Kostiantynivka, and had moved to within roughly 7 to 12 km of Sloviansk and Kramatorsk. Reuters could not verify the battlefield claims, but the statement marked a significant Russian assertion of momentum against Ukraine's key Donbas defensive line.
dl: MODERATE — Russia claims new pressure on Ukraine's main Donbas defensive belt

title: EU signals imminent approval of a 90 billion euro loan package for Ukraine
detail: EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas said she expected positive decisions within a day on a 90 billion euro loan for Ukraine, while ministers also pushed forward work on a 20th sanctions package against Russia. The package would be a major financial and political reinforcement for Kyiv at a moment of acute wartime funding pressure.
dl: LIGHT — Major EU financing package moves toward approval for Ukraine

title: Ukrainian drones hit a Druzhba-linked oil pumping station in Samara region
detail: Ukrainian drones struck an oil-pumping and dispatch facility in Prosvet, Samara region, which a Ukrainian security source said was part of the Druzhba pipeline supply chain. Reuters reported the strike caused a fire and reportedly damaged five large crude storage tanks, highlighting Kyiv's continued effort to disrupt Russian oil logistics tied to the war economy.
dl: MODERATE — Ukrainian deep strike targets Russian pipeline-linked oil logistics

title: ISW says Ukrainian forces likely liberated Andriivka in northern Sumy Oblast
detail: The Institute for the Study of War reported that geolocated footage published on April 21 showed Ukrainian forces holding positions in northern Andriivka, indicating that Ukraine likely retook the village north of Sumy city. The assessed advance marked a localized Ukrainian territorial gain along the northern border while Russian units continued infiltration attempts in the area.
dl: LIGHT — Localized Ukrainian advance near the Sumy border line

title: Ukraine warns Russian missiles and drones are repeatedly flying near Chornobyl and Khmelnytskyi nuclear sites
detail: Reuters reported Ukrainian Prosecutor General Ruslan Kravchenko saying Russia has repeatedly routed drones and Kinzhal missiles close to the Chornobyl site and the Khmelnytskyi nuclear plant, with dozens of flights recorded and several missile impacts near Khmelnytskyi. Kyiv framed the pattern as intimidation that raises the risk of a nuclear safety incident far beyond the immediate battlefield.
dl: LIGHT — Ukraine flags repeated Russian missile and drone routes near nuclear infrastructure

title: EU ambassadors approve 90 billion euro Ukraine loan and a new sanctions package on Russia
detail: Reuters reported that EU ambassadors approved the disbursement of a promised 90 billion euro loan to Ukraine and agreed a new package of sanctions against Russia after Hungary lifted its veto. The move represented one of the bloc's most consequential recent financial and political decisions in support of Kyiv.
dl: LIGHT — EU clears major new financing and sanctions package tied to the war
